title: TERRITORY: Qix Clone
pitch: A fast-paced arcade game where the player draws lines to claim territory while evading enemy stalkers. Control a cursor around the arena edge, hold SPACE to draw claim lines, and complete loops to flip territory to your color. Two enemy types escalate difficulty: Stalkers chase your line, Patrollers block safe zones. Simple one-button mechanic, intense skill-based gameplay, procedural difficulty curve with natural stopping points.
core_loop: Move cursor around arena boundary with arrow keys/WASD at 240px/s
Hold SPACE to draw claim line perpendicular toward opposite edge; release to commit
Avoid Stalker enemies that pathfind to your active line and Patrollers that block claimed territory
Complete line without collision to instantly flip interior to your color and gain score
Repeat until death (enemy collision) or arena >90% claimed; escalate enemy count per wave
controls: ARROW KEYS or WASD: Move cursor around arena edge
SPACE (hold): Extend claim line perpendicular to edge; release to finalize
quality_bar: Input latency <16ms: All movement polled per frame, no event queue delays
Pixel-perfect collision: AABB checks for cursor-vs-enemy and line-vs-enemy detection
Readable threats: Enemy movement trails visible, color-coded by type (Stalker=magenta, Patroller=cyan)
Fair difficulty curve: Enemies spawn in unclaimed territory only, giving 2s warning before entering claimed space

files.name: main.py
files.purpose: Game loop orchestrator, state machine, and entry point. Polls input per frame, updates game systems with delta time, and delegates rendering. No external assets or file I/O.
public_api: QixGame class with __init__(), handle_events(), update(dt), render(), run()

files.name: config.py
files.purpose: Centralized constants: screen dimensions, FPS, player/enemy speeds, colors, and arena boundaries. Zero logic.
public_api: SCREEN_WIDTH, SCREEN_HEIGHT, FPS, PLAYER_SPEED, ENEMY_SPEED, STALKER_SPEED, PATROLLER_SPEED, LINE_WIDTH, GRID_MARGIN, GRID_CELL_SIZE, COLLISION_RADIUS
COLOR_BG, COLOR_PLAYER_LINE, COLOR_PLAYER_CURSOR, COLOR_STALKER, COLOR_PATROLLER, COLOR_CLAIMED_P1, COLOR_GRID

files.name: game_state.py
files.purpose: Central game data container and state machine. Tracks player position, trails, claimed territory, enemy positions, score, level, and game state. Exposes simple queries for collision and level progression checks.
public_api: GameState enum (MENU, PLAYING, CAUGHT, LEVEL_COMPLETE, GAME_OVER)
GameData class with fields: state, score, level, wave, claimed_percentage, player_pos, enemies (list), player_trail, claimed_cells
Methods: reset_level(), add_claimed(cells), check_collision(), update_claimed_percentage(), get_enemy_by_id()

files.name: player.py
files.purpose: Player cursor logic: movement along arena boundary, trail recording, and line-drawing state. Updates position based on input and delta time, enforces boundary constraints, and manages active draw line.

files.name: enemy.py
files.purpose: Enemy AI and movement for both Stalker (pathfinding to player line) and Patroller (bouncing in claimed territory). Each enemy tracks its own trail, updates position with delta time, and returns movement vectors for next frame.
public_api: Enemy base class with __init__(pos, speed, enemy_type)
update(dt, player_pos, player_trail, claimed_cells, boundaries) -> new_pos

files.name: territory.py
files.purpose: Geometric algorithms for territory claim detection. Implements loop closure detection via grid snapping and polygon ray-casting, flood-fill to identify claimed cells, and Bresenham line rasterization to discretize drawn lines.
public_api: detect_closed_loop(player_trail, qix_trail, grid_size, boundaries) -> (is_closed: bool, claimed_cells: set)

files.name: render.py
files.purpose: All Pygame rendering: background, grid, trails (player and enemy), claimed territory fills, sprites (cursor and enemies), HUD (score, level, percentage), and state overlays. Zero game logic, pure drawing delegation.

files.name: audio.py
files.purpose: Synthesized audio feedback: line-complete chord (major third), enemy spawn alerts (3-tone descending), death buzz, and background ambience. All sounds generated via numpy + Pygame mixer, no external files.
